questions.question: The new `clearPendingDirectInvite` dep is added to `ProfileHandlerDeps` (`profileHandler.ts:19-25`) and threaded through `registerHandlers.ts` (`:18-65,93-99`). Confirm it is actually WIRED (a real function reference passed at the registration site, not a placeholder `async () => {}` or `undefined` cast to satisfy the type) — run `grep -n "clearPendingDirectInvite" app/src/lib/marmot/handlers/registerHandlers.ts` and confirm the wired value is `pendingDirectInviteStorage`'s real `del`-equivalent export, not a stub.

`profileHandler.ts`'s header comment states 'zero imports from `app/src/context/`... all IDB and state-setter deps received via injection.' Confirm `clearPendingDirectInvite` is added to `ProfileHandlerDeps` as an injected function (matching the file's existing `mergeMemberProfile`/`notifyProfileObserved`/etc. shape) rather than a direct import of `pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts` into `profileHandler.ts` itself. Run `grep -n "pendingDirectInviteStorage" app/src/lib/marmot/handlers/profileHandler.ts` and confirm zero matches — the import belongs only in `registerHandlers.ts`'s wiring site.

Could `ProfileHandler` be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its `dependencies_allowed` list? If not, which cross-module dependencies exist and do they flow through declared seam contracts in architecture.md? Concretely: `profileHandler.ts`'s only new cross-module surface is the `PendingDirectInviteClearFn` seam (consumed here, defined by S1) — confirm this story's tests can inject a mock `clearPendingDirectInvite` without importing S1's real store, and that the handler's existing `mergeMemberProfile`/`recordRequestAnswered`/`notifyProfileObserved` deps are untouched by this diff (a regression check that this story only ADDS one dep, never modifies the shape of the existing four).

Provide unit tests for both branches of `handle()`'s new gate: (a) `profilePayload.signedEvent` present with `signedEvent.pubkey` equal to the marked pubkey → `clearPendingDirectInvite(ctx.groupId, authorPubkey)` is called, asserted for BOTH `mergeMemberProfile` returning `true` and returning `false` (two separate test cases, not one — a naive `if (merged) clear()` gate would pass only the `true` case); (b) a rumor-only payload with no `signedEvent` → `clearPendingDirectInvite` is NOT called, even though `rumor.pubkey` equals the marked pubkey (proving the gate does not fall back to `rumor.pubkey`).

Architecture.md's implementation constraint 2 states the clear gate must be 'relay-on-behalf safe: require the signed envelope, do not fall back to `rumor.pubkey`' — a relayer forwarding a rumor on behalf of another member has `rumor.pubkey` equal to the RELAYER, not the profile's subject, per this file's existing `authorPubkey = profilePayload.signedEvent?.pubkey ?? rumor.pubkey` line (`:33`). Confirm the marker-clear gate reads `signedEvent.pubkey` directly (or the already-derived `authorPubkey` ONLY when `profilePayload.signedEvent` is truthy) and never clears a marker keyed to `rumor.pubkey` when `signedEvent` is absent — a bug here would let a malicious or buggy relayer clear an invitee's marker by forwarding an unsigned rumor claiming to be that invitee, letting a still-pending invite masquerade as confirmed.

Confirm this story does not modify `pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts`'s `del()` implementation (S1's exclusive scope, per this story's `excludes`) and does not touch marker-write call sites at `InviteMemberModal.tsx`/`profile.tsx` (S7/S8's exclusive scope). This story's diff should be confined to `profileHandler.ts` and `registerHandlers.ts`.

AC-MARKER-5 requires the marker for `${groupId}:${pubkey}` to be cleared 'regardless of whether `mergeMemberProfile` returns `true` or `false`' when the profile handler processes an own-signed profile for a marked pubkey. Provide the exact line in `handle()` where the clear call is placed RELATIVE to the `merged` boolean check (`:38-42`) — it must NOT be nested inside `if (merged) { ... }`, since that branch is specifically for `recordRequestAnswered`, a DIFFERENT side effect with different gating semantics. Provide two passing test assertions, one for each `merged` value, both confirming the clear was called.

AC-MARKER-6 requires the marker MUST NOT be cleared 'on the basis of `rumor.pubkey` alone' when the payload lacks a signed envelope. Provide a unit test where `profilePayload.signedEvent` is `undefined`/absent, `rumor.pubkey` equals the marked pubkey, and assert `clearPendingDirectInvite` is never invoked for this call — distinct from AC-MARKER-5's test (which requires a present `signedEvent`), so a single shared fixture cannot satisfy both ACs.

The implemented clear block wraps `deps.clearPendingDirectInvite(ctx.groupId, authorPubkey)` in try/catch and does `console.warn` on failure (profileHandler.ts, new block right after `deps.setProfileVersion((v) => v + 1)` and before `if (merged) { ... recordRequestAnswered ... }`). Confirm this try/catch does not also accidentally swallow a throw from `deps.setProfileVersion` or `deps.mergeMemberProfile` (i.e. the catch's scope is exactly the one `await deps.clearPendingDirectInvite(...)` call, not a wider block) — a catch with too broad a scope would silently hide a merge failure as a 'marker clear failed' log line, misleading anyone debugging a lost profile update.

`app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx` now has two distinct call sites touching the pending-direct-invite marker store: the new `clearPendingDirectInvite` (singular, per-key) wired into `buildDispatcher`'s profile deps (this story), and the pre-existing `clearPendingDirectInvitesForGroup` (plural, per-group bulk) wired into the `leaveGroup` useCallback (S3, already present in the working tree before this story started — confirmed via `git show HEAD:app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx` showing zero occurrences of either name, i.e. both are uncommitted). Confirm these two functions are genuinely distinct exports from `pendingDirectInviteStorage.ts` (not the same function aliased twice) and that this story's diff to `MarmotContext.tsx` is confined to the two-line addition in the profile-deps section (import + `buildDispatcher` field) — i.e. this story did not modify, duplicate, or interact with the S3 `leaveGroup` wiring.

Beyond the 'no signedEvent at all' case, the implementation added a distinct relay-on-behalf test asserting `clearPendingDirectInvite` is called with the AUTHOR's pubkey and explicitly NOT with the relayer's (`rumor.pubkey`) pubkey, using `makeSignedProfileRumor({ rumorSenderPubkey: PEER_PUBKEY, authorPubkey: AUTHOR_PUBKEY })`. Confirm this test would actually fail under a buggy implementation that derives the clear-target from `rumor.pubkey` instead of `authorPubkey`/`signedEvent.pubkey` when both are present but differ — i.e. that PEER_PUBKEY and AUTHOR_PUBKEY are distinct constants and the assertion `not.toHaveBeenCalledWith(GROUP_ID, PEER_PUBKEY)` is meaningful (not vacuously true because the mock was never called with any group/pubkey pair at all).
